hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by enhancing hydrophobic complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 pockets, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target interactions by avoiding charge-based interactions with SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong binding but lack sufficient SSTR2 selectivity due to conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeting non-pharmacophore positions with mutations that break these interactions is critical for achieving the desired selectivity margin.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target interactions are likely to occur (e.g., conserved residues in SSTR3/SSTR5).
Propose mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target binding by breaking conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5.
